summary:
The earliest safe diagnosis of pregnancy requires visualization of a viable fetus through transvaginal ultrasound, typically around 6-8 weeks, rather than relying solely on hCG measurements. Although beta-hCG rises soon after conception and can be detected earlier, isolated hCG levels lack specificity and cannot rule out ectopic or failed pregnancies, thus failing to provide a safe or definitive diagnosis without corroborating imaging.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$
